channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: White plastic builders' waste fly-tipped outside nos. 48 / 49 Ashburnham Grove SE10 8UJ, 8th July
item.description:
This piece of junk had been dumped at this spot a week ago, then disappeared and has been fly-tipped again outside these properties.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashburnham Grove, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees and shrubs blocking pavement, 8th July
item.description:
Shockingly overgrown condition of Vicarage Drive "gardens" mean their trees and shrubs blocking pavement. Japanese knotweed believed to be on site. Makes Rectory Road look uncared for. Pavement upswept and full of litter. Leak on pavement reported to Thames Water 7/7/26.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 77, Rectory Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass left on pathway, 8th July
item.description:
House on the end of guildroad has dumped grass and tree cutting on to pathway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Boundary Street, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin / litter in street, 8th July
item.description:
Litter bin at 'W' bus stop used by locals for domestic rubbish. Often overflowing and litter builds up litter on pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 77, Rectory Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vegetation blocking pavement, 8th July
item.description:
Obstructs prams and wheelchairs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Kenley Close, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recycling banks adj to health clinic, 8th July
item.description:
• Please give brief details of your complaint or compliment below: There are several recycling waste bins positioned next to Bromley Healthcare Clinic at 40 Kimmeridge Road. The recycling bins are often overflowing out onto the pavement. The types of waste being left, is not appropriate for the bins, this includes food waste, a gas bottle a gas bottle behind one of the bins, there are items of furniture being left outside of the bins. There is a cause for concern for the types of waste being left outside the bins, as this could attract rats and mice. There is an impact for patients attending appointments at Mottingham Clinic. • What action (if any) would you like us to take? When the waste is collected, sweep the area and remove any items that are outside of the recycling bins. Disinfect the area regularly. Consider moving the recycling bins to the rear of the car park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dittisham Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: BT manhole lid collapsed and damaged and formed a hole, 8th July
item.description:
On the corner of Frensham St next to Travis Perkins, there is a BT manhole lid on the footpath that has collapsed due to the ground next to it forming a void, making it unstable. This poses a Health and Safety risk to public. Ground beside of manhole lid to be reinstated to make safe and cover stable.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Frensham Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead tree dumped, 8th July
item.description:
A large, dead tree has been deposited on the pavement at the top of Tatnell Road/corner of Grierson Road. This has been reported to Lewisham’s ‘Love Clean Streets’ and is marked there as case closed! The dead tree is still there! It has, admittedly, been moved along the pavement, but the same debris is still within 15 metres of where it was reported. Perhaps a nudge via Fix My Street will get it removed?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tatnell Road, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous pot hole, 8th July
item.description:
Large pot hole in the road about foot and half wide stretches across from house 9 to opposite house 16 -
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Dalmeny Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree number 40, 8th July
item.description:
Lower branches overhanging paths, tree 40 shoulder of mutton green Welling

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bellegrove Road, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light, 8th July
item.description:
Street light on 24/7 Harbex Close in front of first garage block
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harbex Close, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dozens of bikes causing blocked pavement, 8th July
item.description:
Dozens of lime and forest bikes are abandoned fallen over on this corner
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Gracechurch Street, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very dirty street, please respect the schedule!, 8th July
item.description:
As always, Seymour Villas and other parts of Anerley, Penge, and Crystal Palace are not being cleaned properly. The service provider is not cleaning Seymour Villas twice a week as required, which is unacceptable. Will we receive a reduction in our council tax, which increases every year? We pay for this service, yet our area is consistently neglected. We should not have to submit complaints every week just to ensure the service is carried out. We want to live in clean streets and neighbourhoods, just like those in Bromley and Beckenham.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Seymour Villas, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping in alleyway behind 2b Bellegrove Road, 8th July
item.description:
Flytipping in alleyway behind 2b Bellegrove Road - can this be removed please - there are 2 mattresses, cardboard boxes, pin board and other rubbish. This is ongoing issue and they would like to know what can be done about this.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bellegrove Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 8th July
item.description:
There’s been rubbish in this former garden area for some time now
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43-67, Scylla Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 8th July
item.description:
2nd abandoned car (previously reported another car next to it) not moved in over a month and wondering they’ve been stolen

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Roberton Drive, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Residents of Oulton Close SE28 8LT dumping their bins and rubbish on Rollesby Way. Always dirty and fouling the environment, 8th July
item.description:
Greenwich environmental department need to help see to it that the lanlords and residents in oulton close Thamesmead manage their bin property. The residents in oulton close dont take back their bins after bin collector and they usually use the wrong bin and always put the bin on Rollesby way and fool the arears. See the picture. This has been ongoing. Greenwich council should introduce penalt charge for this kind of behaviour because it always mess up the area for other. Most of them are stucked inside their close but leave others to suffer for their action. It is not fare at all. Often is other people that report the mess they make

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rollesby Way, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass awes, 8th July
item.description:
The grass hasn’t been cut for several weeks —or months— at all the Consort Estate. Now there are too many long awes in all the green areas
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Philip Walk, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 8th July
item.description:
Car has been left here and not moved in over a month. Wondering if it’s been stolen and dumped. There are actually 2 cards side by side (will report other car separately)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Roberton Drive, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council bins covered in graffiti, 8th July
item.description:
Contributing to lots of graffiti on this square. Please can this be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cleaver Square, Kennington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
